option.name: originalContent
option.value:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -e
# Resolve the directory this script lives in, regardless of where it's called from APP_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)" cd "$APP_DIR"
# Activate the local venv so all dependencies are available source "$APP_DIR/venv/bin/activate"
python3 -u MWSS_Field_Manager.py

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Resolve the directory this script lives in, regardless of where it's called from APP_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)" cd "$APP_DIR"
LOG="$APP_DIR/field_manager.log" echo "=== $(date) ===" >> "$LOG" echo "APP_DIR: $APP_DIR" >> "$LOG"
# Activate the local venv if it exists, otherwise fall back to system python3 if [ -f "$APP_DIR/venv/bin/activate" ]; then source "$APP_DIR/venv/bin/activate" echo "venv activated" >> "$LOG" else echo "WARNING: venv not found at $APP_DIR/venv — using system python3" >> "$LOG" echo "To create it run: python3 -m venv $APP_DIR/venv && $APP_DIR/venv/bin/pip install -r $APP_DIR/requirements.txt" fi
python3 -u MWSS_Field_Manager.py >> "$LOG" 2>&1 EXIT_CODE=$?
if [ $EXIT_CODE -ne 0 ]; then echo "ERROR: MWSS_Field_Manager.py exited with code $EXIT_CODE" >> "$LOG" # Show a visible error popup so it doesn't silently disappear which zenity &>/dev/null && zenity --error --text="Field Manager crashed (exit $EXIT_CODE).\nSee log:\n$LOG" --title="Field Manager Error" fi
